system_description.overview: Counsel is an AI-powered workflow management platform and operating system for law firms that captures emails, documents, and internal conversations to maximize billable hours and streamline legal operations. It uses LLM technology, RAG-powered document retrieval, and agentic workflow automation to transform law firm efficiency.

products.Legal Workflow Management Platform: Centralized system of record for law firms with email integration, document management, task tracking, client and project management
products.AI-Powered Legal Intelligence: Automated workflow capture using LLMs, RAG-powered document and knowledge retrieval, agentic workflow automation with rich process data collection
products.Implementation and Onboarding Services: Dedicated onboarding and platform setup for boutique law firms with custom workflow configuration
products.Strategic Partnership Support: Embedded support via Microsoft Teams integration, workflow optimization consulting

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — AWS is a subservice organization)
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical office or servers

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Production VPC and Staging VPC separated; Vector DB + Indexing VPC separate; segmented VPCs with ACLs; network segmentation by sensitivity and criticality
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls on application gateway and production network; firewall rules reviewed annually; AWS security groups and network ACLs
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS/IPS via AWS (intrusion detection and prevention tools configured)
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host VPN for administrative/management access
network_architecture.tls: TLS for all client HTTPS requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; email scanning for malicious scripts
network_architecture.topology: Client HTTPS Request → Production VPC (ECS containers, AWS containers, Postgres RDS, Redis cache, S3, DocVPC containers) → AWS Endpoint Service Core → Vector DB + Indexing VPC (RAG Service, Vector DB EKS cluster) → Refining VPC (Refining Service) → us-west-2 (AWS Bedrock). Staging VPC mirrors production. Management connection via SSH bastion.

application_architecture.api_style: REST and/or gRPC (HTTP or gRPC noted in diagram for inter-service communication)
application_architecture.environments: Production VPC and Staging VPC fully separated; development/test environments separate from production

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (Active Directory, server and database accounts; AWS password policy enforced)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all remote access and administrative accounts
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; lockout after failed attempts

encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: SCM (name not disclosed; MFA enforced; source code changes logged, timestamped, attributed)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge to master/main requires additional approval; branch rules configured; developers cannot push directly to production
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as code; configuration management tool for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management; CTO performs monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (name not disclosed) with predefined threshold alerts; continuous infrastructure performance monitoring
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level; log access restricted; logs retained per policy
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; alerts tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure — compute (ECS, EKS), database (RDS), storage (S3), networking, security (IDS/IPS), AI (Bedrock LLM)
LLM inference for AI-powered legal intelligence features
Client communication channel integration for Strategic Partnership Support

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution on all desktops/laptops and production servers; continuous auto-scan; weekly AV definition updates for production servers; anti-malware and Trojan protection; email scanning
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning; self-assessment scan prior to every production release

untestable: CC2.2.3 — significant personnel changes not made during engagement period (no events to sample)
CC6.5.2 — no customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 — no security incidents reported during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — cybersecurity insurance control untestable; no cyber incidents during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — physical access delegated to AWS (subservice); AWS responsible for data center security
A1.2 — environmental protections delegated to AWS and building management
Processing Integrity criteria — not relevant to system
Privacy criteria — not relevant to system
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) established with semiannual review cadence
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meets weekly on security operations
Vendor management includes quarterly risk assessment for non-SOC vendors
Multi-AZ DR with failover by spinning up servers across all AZs in AWS
Separate staging VPC from production VPC

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-region (us-east-1 primary + us-west-2 for Bedrock), multi-AZ, IaC baseline configs, separate staging VPC, ECS+EKS containers, Postgres RDS, Redis, S3. No CDN and no stated WAF limit the score slightly.

application_architecture.rationale: Microservices with ECS + EKS, separate production/staging environments, branch protection with required approvals, Vector DB + RAG service shows AI architecture maturity. Languages/frameworks not disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: Postgres RDS + Vector DB + Redis cache + S3 — multi-tier data architecture. Daily backups with multi-AZ, KMS encryption, per-tenant data segmentation confirmed. No data warehouse.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS via AWS, monthly vuln scans + pre-release self-assessment, annual external pen test, antivirus, MFA for all remote access, cybersecurity insurance, content filtering, firewall reviews. No WAF named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with rollback capability, ECS+EKS orchestration, separate staging/production VPCs, branch protection, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews, weekly ITLC meetings, daily patch compliance scans. CI/CD tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy, annual review and testing confirmed, daily backups, multi-AZ failover via spinning up servers across all AZs. No stated RTO/RPO and no multi-region DR.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS is the dominant single provider (infrastructure + Bedrock LLM). Microsoft Teams is a significant integration. Vendor management program with quarterly reviews for non-SOC vendors shows maturity despite concentration.

overall.rationale: Strong SOC 2 Type 2 with 3-month operational evidence and zero exceptions. Multi-region AWS, sophisticated AI architecture (RAG + Vector DB + Bedrock), robust governance (RGEC + ITLC). Gaps: no WAF, no stated RTO/RPO, AWS concentration risk, unnamed CI/CD tooling.

red_flags: No WAF disclosed — application layer attack protection gap
No RTO/RPO stated — availability commitments unquantified
Heavy AWS concentration including LLM inference via AWS Bedrock — critical multi-layer dependency
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool and monitoring platform not named
No CDN mentioned — potential latency for geographically distributed law firms
No multi-region failover — DR limited to multi-AZ within us-east-1
Security criteria only — Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy not independently audited
3-month audit window (Apr-Jul 2025) is minimum — limited operational evidence period
green_flags: SOC 2 Type 2 with 3-month operational evidence — controls tested and verified operating
Multi-region AWS deployment (us-east-1 + us-west-2) for Bedrock services
Sophisticated AI architecture: EKS Vector DB + RAG Service + AWS Bedrock LLM
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with semiannual review
IT Leadership Committee (ITLC) meeting weekly on security operations
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning + pre-release self-assessment scans
Daily backups with multi-AZ and KMS encryption
Quarterly access reviews with 1-business-day revocation SLA
key_observations: GetCounsel is a legal-vertical AI platform — handles attorney-client privileged communications and legal documents, making security posture critical for customer trust
AWS Bedrock integration in us-west-2 as a separate region shows deliberate cloud architecture for AI inference workloads
Vector DB on EKS cluster is a distinct architectural component enabling RAG — indicates substantial AI engineering investment
Staging VPC fully separate from Production VPC — stronger environment isolation than typical ACCORP-template companies in this batch
ACCORP Partners auditor — same firm as other companies in this batch but this is a Type 2 report with actual control testing
PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383 notation on page 7 appears to be an auditor license number
No physical office — fully remote/cloud-native operation reduces physical security risk surface
